articles.title: NYPD officer assigned to Mayor Mamdani’s security suspended after off-duty shooting
articles.summary: A New York City police officer allegedly shot a man in the head while off-duty in the Bronx and has been suspended without pay as the department investigates.

articles.title: Trump continues to push for release of Tina Peters as Colorado governor weighs clemency
articles.summary: President Donald Trump continues to pressure Colorado's governor to free Tina Peters, who is serving a nine-year sentence for a security breach of voting systems.

articles.title: Colorado school staffer accused of sexually abusing 13-year-old student, now on the run
articles.summary: A Colorado school staffer allegedly abused a 13-year-old student for months. She is now on the run, facing 12 felony charges including sexual assault on a child.

articles.title: State Department to ask for bonds of up to $15,000 for visa applications from a dozen more countries
articles.summary: The State Department expanded visa bond requirements to 12 more countries, whose citizens must post up to $15,000 bonds before traveling to the U.S.

articles.title: Joseph Duggar arrested for allegedly molesting 9-year-old girl during Florida vacation
articles.summary: The former "19 Kids and Counting" star was arrested and charged this week with allegedly molesting a 9-year-old girl during a 2020 Florida vacation.

articles.title: LSU star Flau’jae Johnson carries bearded dragon into final NCAA Tournament run of college career
articles.summary: Flau’jae Johnson will take the floor in the NCAA Tournament for the final time Friday. Johnson, a college basketball national champion, reflected on her four-year career at LSU.

articles.title: Former counterterrorism chief Joe Kent under FBI investigation for alleged classified leaks
articles.summary: FBI investigating former counterterror chief Joe Kent over alleged classified leak, sources tell Fox News Digital, with probe predating his abrupt resignation.

articles.title: California Democrats distance themselves from famed pro-union activist after bombshell report
articles.summary: Sexual abuse allegations against Cesar Chavez prompted several California leaders including Gov. Gavin Newsom to distance themselves from the labor activist.

articles.title: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'misfunctioning,' not controlling regime: sources
articles.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, took power after his father Ali Khamenei was killed in an Israeli strike, but Israeli intelligence says he lacks control over the regime.

articles.title: College Republicans of America stands by new political director amid scrutiny of past controversial comments
articles.summary: The College Republicans of America face backlash after appointing Kai Schwemmer as political director after his past controversial statements came to light.

articles.title: Immigration judge orders deportation of NYC Council employee after ICE arrest; city leaders push back
articles.summary: An NYC Council employee is facing deportation despite officials claiming legal work authorization. An Immigration judge ordered the employee's removal amid the controversy.

articles.title: Eagles acquire veteran quarterback Andy Dalton from Panthers with backup role in sight
articles.summary: The Eagles acquired veteran quarterback Andy Dalton from the Panthers for a seventh-round pick to potentially back up Jalen Hurts. The 38-year-old will battle Tanner McKee.

articles.title: Millionaire developer found dead in ritzy beach home; bizarrely dressed suspect arrested after standoff
articles.summary: A millionaire developer, 92, was found dead in a Southern California beach home after a bizarrely dressed suspect surrendered after an hours-long SWAT standoff, authorities say.

articles.title: Jamie Lee Curtis slams 'abomination' Trump for trying to 'erase' diversity 'from our language'
articles.summary: "Halloween" actress Jamie Lee Curtis claimed President Donald Trump was trying to "erase" diversity from language and suggested the Constitution be "amended."

articles.title: After Michigan attack, Jewish teens show antisemitism won’t define them
articles.summary: After Oct. 7, Jewish youth organizations shifted from confronting antisemitism to strengthening Jewish identity through community and tradition.

articles.title: GOP overperforms in Virginia special election, fueling early momentum talk in blue-trending state
articles.summary: Republican Andrew Rice won a special election in Virginia Tuesday night, sparking talk about Gov. Abigail Spanberger's policies driving Republicans to the polls.

articles.title: GOP blocks Booker-led push to curb Trump's military authority in Iran
articles.summary: Senate Democrats plan multiple war powers resolutions to challenge President Trump's Iran military operations in a bid to force public testimony from top administration officials.

articles.title: Hero police officers storm inferno to save 5 passengers from fireball crash
articles.summary: Michigan police officers heroically rescued five people from a fiery car crash in Canton Township, and four officers were injured during the rescue.

articles.title: Iran’s hidden mountain nuclear site raises urgent threat, must be ‘neutralized': reports
articles.summary: Andrea Stricker warns the U.S. must neutralize Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site and secure uranium stockpiles before ending combat operations.

articles.title: Former NFL running back Boston Scott calls it quits at 30, reveals feelings of 'anger, bitterness'
articles.summary: Philadelphia Eagles player Boston Scott has officially ended his NFL career three years after his final game. The running back reflected on his journey from walk-on to NFL success.

articles.title: MS NOW leans into its liberal identity by rolling back on straight news programs, insiders say
articles.summary: MS NOW staffers say the network is leaning into its liberal identity after announcing an overhaul of its daytime programming and ending straight news shows.

articles.title: Sen Rand Paul says Trump’s DHS pick, Markwayne Mullin, is 'unfit' for the job after heated hearing exchange
articles.summary: Sen. Rand Paul, R-Ky., criticized President Donald Trump’s Department of Homeland Security nominee, Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., on "America Reports."

articles.title: NBA All-Star calls out March Madness fans for making too many brackets: 'Just so lame'
articles.summary: NBA star Tyrese Haliburton sparked a March Madness debate with his bold bracket advice of making one and standing on it. The Pacers guard picked Iowa State to win it all.

articles.title: GOP gubernatorial hopeful hails legendary golfer for hefty campaign donation: 'Incredibly honored'
articles.summary: Phil Mickelson made a rare $39,200 donation to Republican Steve Hilton's California gubernatorial campaign, backing candidate's opposition to climate mandates.

articles.title: Uma Thurman reveals why she never lived in Los Angeles
articles.summary: "Kill Bill" star Uma Thurman chose family over Hollywood proximity, admitting that staying in New York prevented industry integration she now wishes she'd had.

articles.title: Gabbard sidesteps Iran ‘imminent threat’ claim under Senate grilling
articles.summary: Intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard refuses to confirm Trump's Iran threat claims during tense Senate hearing, sparking heated debate over assessment.

articles.title: Almost 200 House Dems vote against deporting people who commit welfare fraud
articles.summary: House Republicans approved a bill targeting noncitizens who commit fraud despite Democratic opposition. The measure faces an uphill battle in the Senate.

articles.title: ‘How do you sleep at night?' Moreno slams ‘disgraceful’ shutdown leaving 260,000 workers without pay
articles.summary: Sen. Bernie Moreno slams Democrats as 'disgusting' over government shutdown blame, highlighting 260,000 DHS employees working without paychecks during crisis.

articles.title: GOP governors, AGs back Trump SAVE Act push, warn system gives ‘undue influence’ to states with illegal aliens
articles.summary: Republican governors and attorneys general back citizenship verification through the SAVE America Act for federal elections, arguing gaps in voter registration create unfair influence.

articles.title: Strikes may set Iran back but likely won't end nuclear program, UN watchdog chief warns
articles.summary: A U.N. nuclear chief says Iran's nuclear program cannot be eliminated by airstrikes alone, with uranium stockpiles and underground sites remaining intact.

articles.title: House panel summons Soros-backed Fairfax prosecutor over releases tied to violent illegal immigrant cases
articles.summary: House panel summons Fairfax sheriff and Soros-funded prosecutor over alleged failures to prosecute violent illegal immigrants in sanctuary county.

articles.title: Powell says he has 'no intention of leaving' Fed during DOJ investigation
articles.summary: Jerome Powell said he does not plan to leave the Federal Reserve during the Department of Justice investigation and would serve as chair pro tempore if needed.

articles.title: White House says ‘no one’ changing Trump enforcement agenda in response to Angel Mom appeal to Mullin
articles.summary: The White House and DHS issued a response to a request by Angel Mom Angie Morfin, whose 13-year-old son, Ruben, was executed by an illegal alien gang member in California.

articles.title: UN nuclear chief warns strike near Iran reactor risks crossing ‘reddest line’
articles.summary: UN nuclear watchdog warns projectile strike near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant crosses "reddest line" of nuclear safety as regional conflict intensifies.

articles.title: Scalise accuses Democrats of reviving 'defund the police' push with DHS funding gambit
articles.summary: GOP leaders Steve Scalise and Mike Johnson blast a Democratic move to bypass leadership and fund the DHS while excluding ICE and Border Patrol law enforcement.

articles.title: Sen Mullin gets emotional recounting Trump's kindness to his son
articles.summary: Sen. Markwayne Mullin fought back tears during his confirmation hearing as he revealed how President Donald Trump supported his family after his son's brain injury.

articles.title: Lone wolf attackers pose most likely terror threat to US homeland, intelligence report reiterates
articles.summary: The 2026 U.S. threat assessment highlights lone wolf extremists as the primary terrorism risk, with online radicalization fueling attacks.

articles.title: NYC spends more per homeless person than a typical household earns in a year, data shows
articles.summary: NYC homelessness spending skyrockets 262% to $368M since 2019, yet street homeless population grows 26%, raising serious questions about program effectiveness.

articles.title: Dem-backed 'social justice' law put Virginia's ODU campus at risk before attack, former AG argues
articles.summary: Former Virginia AG Miyares argues Democrats' background check law prevented ODU from knowing about a shooter's terrorism conviction, calling it an unsafe policy.

articles.title: Progressive influencer erupts after Illinois primary loss, drops profane anti-Trump, -ICE rant
articles.summary: Progressive candidate Kat Abughazaleh delivered an expletive-filled concession speech after losing the Illinois Democratic primary, targeting President Donald Trump and ICE.

articles.title: FBI director ticks off terror threats foiled by agents
articles.summary: The FBI used expanded online surveillance and biometric tracking to prevent December terrorist attacks, including a planned bombing and New Year's Eve events, FBI Director Kash Patel said.

articles.title: McConnell claims Joe Kent's resignation letter contained 'virulent anti-Semitism'
articles.summary: GOP Sen. Mitch McConnell asserted that Joe Kent's letter of resignation from the the National Counterterrorism Center director role displayed "virulent anti-Semitism."

articles.title: Leaked teachers' union K-12 training presentation rails against Trump administration, red states
articles.summary: Nation's largest teachers union faces criticism for political activism after leaked training docs called democracy "at stake" and targeted GOP states.

articles.title: Russia ships fuel to Cuba using 'spoofing' tactic challenging Trump embargo: reports
articles.summary: Hong Kong-flagged tanker allegedly delivered 190,000 barrels of Russian oil to Cuba using AIS spoofing and ship-to-ship transfers to evade U.S. sanctions.

articles.title: Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez replaces sanctioned loyalist defense minister with military intel head
articles.summary: Acting president Delcy Rodriguez has appointed Gustavo Gonzalez Lopez to lead the defense ministry, a move to consolidate military control.

articles.title: One dead after cable car detaches, plummets at Swiss ski resort
articles.summary: A 61-year-old woman died Wednesday after a gondola detached and crashed on a snowy mountainside in Engelberg, Switzerland, authorities said.

articles.title: Former Assad-era prison chief convicted of torture in US federal court, marking a historic first
articles.summary: Samir Ousman Alsheikh, a former Syrian prison chief, was found guilty of torture and immigration fraud charges, becoming the first Assad-era official convicted in a U.S. court.

articles.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing hundreds in Kabul hospital strike
articles.summary: Reported airstrike on Afghanistan hospital allegedly kills 400+ civilians, sparking widespread criticism over muted international response to the attack.

articles.title: Belgium deploys military to guard Jewish sites after Iran-linked group claims Europe attacks
articles.summary: Belgium deploys military to protect Jewish communities after explosion outside Liège, Belgium, synagogue. Officials call blast a "despicable antisemitic" act.

articles.title: Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed
articles.summary: Video footage captured fiery destruction and emergency crews responding after an Iranian missile strike near Tel Aviv, Israel, killed two people.

articles.title: Israel kills Iranian intelligence minister who survived initial strike, official says
articles.summary: Iran’s Intelligence Minister Esmaeil Khatib was killed in an Israeli strike, a senior Israeli official told Fox News.

articles.title: Pro-life leader criticizes 'insane' UK bill that would decriminalize certain abortions up until birth
articles.summary: Shawn Carney, CEO of 40 Days for Life, expressed concern over a U.K. bill aimed at shielding women from criminal liability for abortions up until birth, describing it as "insane."

articles.title: Deadly blasts at market and hospital raise fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency in Nigeria
articles.summary: Nigeria suicide bombings in Maiduguri killed at least 23 people and wounded over 100 others in deadly attacks targeting a hospital and market Monday night.

articles.title: Rubio says Cuba needs ‘new people in charge’ as blackouts, unrest grip island
articles.summary: Marco Rubio links U.S. sanctions on Cuba to political change as the island nation faces widespread blackouts and economic crisis affecting millions of citizens.

articles.title: Iran regime hides in bunkers as civilians left exposed without adequate bomb shelters or sirens
articles.summary: Iranian civilians are complaining about a lack of bomb shelters and warning systems as Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps officials hide in bunkers during strikes.

articles.title: Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island, secure uranium or risk ground war escalation
articles.summary: Military analysts discuss three paths for U.S.-Iran conflict: capturing Iran's economic lifeline at Kharg Island, full invasion or securing nuclear sites.

articles.title: EU pushes for end of Iran war in a manner where 'everybody saves face'
articles.summary: The European Union is reportedly consulting with Gulf countries about possible ways Iran, Israel and the U.S. could end the war in a manner where "everybody saves face."

articles.title: Multiple allies decline US calls for Strait of Hormuz support amid rising Middle East tensions
articles.summary: Several U.S. allies are refusing military involvement in securing the Strait of Hormuz, citing diplomacy, legal limits or strategic caution.

articles.title: Top Iranian official, commander killed in strike, Israel defense minister says
articles.summary: Iran's Supreme National Security Council secretary Ali Larijani and Basij Commander Gholamreza Soleimani have been "eliminated," the Israel Defense Forces said.

articles.title: Why Gulf states aren’t joining the war against Iran — despite attacks on their soil
articles.summary: Gulf states maintain restraint despite reported Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar and Kuwait, prioritizing regional stability.

articles.title: Cuba's entire electrical grid collapses, leaving whole island without power
articles.summary: Cuba's power grid suffers total collapse amid fuel shortages and sanctions, leaving the island nation scrambling to restore electricity to millions.

articles.title: Hamas reasserts control in Gaza as Iran war dominates regional attention and global focus
articles.summary: Hamas reportedly reasserts control in Gaza as Iran war shifts international focus, according to analysts raising serious doubts about postwar planning.

articles.title: New terror group with reported Iran ties claims 4 attacks across Europe
articles.summary: Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamiyya attacks on European Jewish institutions may be linked to Iranian proxy networks, according to security analysts and Israeli officials.

articles.title: US warns Iraq must act against Iran-backed militia attacks on American assets
articles.summary: Iran-backed Popular Mobilization Forces have been accused of attacking U.S. assets in Iraq as Prime Minister Mohammed Shia al-Sudani's government told to rein in terror proxies.

articles.title: Trump warns NATO of 'very bad' future if allies don't help secure Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Donald Trump warned NATO allies who fail to help defend the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions over military burden-sharing responsibilities.

articles.title: Pope Leo urges war leaders to halt fighting after deadly strike on school sparks outrage
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV called Sunday for an immediate ceasefire in the war involving Iran, urgently urging world leaders to halt violence after deadly strikes hit schools and civilian areas.

articles.summary: Florida officers used paddleboards to arrest a fleeing suspect who jumped into the Halifax River. Chase Cruz faces loitering and resisting arrest charges.

articles.summary: Dennis Arguello-Acosta was arrested following the death of Deputy Kenneth Lewis, who was struck while assisting drivers on a Texas freeway.

articles.title: Party chaos caught on camera as police push new tactic to scare spring breakers
articles.summary: Spring break Florida crackdown sees Bay County Sheriff Tommy Ford's viral body camera video campaign deter college students from unlawful behavior in Panama City Beach.

articles.title: Mexican man attempts to cross illegally into US via underside of bridge
articles.summary: A Mexican national was arrested after climbing beneath an El Paso overpass near the U.S.-Mexico border in an attempted crossing, according to CBP.

articles.title: Ex-principal sidelined over Charlie Kirk controversy now headed to prison in illicit image case
articles.summary: Former Rainier High principal sentenced to over 5 years for possessing child sexual abuse material. Jeremy Williams must register as sex offender.

articles.title: Realtor's cold case murder finally solved after 15 years, police say
articles.summary: Iowa cold case murder arrest made as Kristin Elizabeth Ramsey charged with first-degree murder in realtor Ashley Okland's 2011 death nearly 15 years later.

articles.title: Homeless man charged with attacking San Francisco mayor’s security detail arrested again days after release
articles.summary: Homeless man accused of attacking San Francisco mayor's security officer arrested again after judge ordered his release, saying he was "violently assaulted."

articles.title: Coast Guard icebreaker rescues snowmobiler stranded on ice during Michigan blizzard
articles.summary: A stranded snowmobiler was rescued by the U.S. Coast Guard near Mackinac Island after getting lost in blizzard conditions and encountering unstable ice and open water.

articles.title: New video shows terror suspect tackled after allegedly throwing bomb at NYC protest outside mayor's residence
articles.summary: Video shows accused terror suspect tackled after allegedly throwing homemade bomb at NYC protest outside Gracie Mansion. NYPD calls it ISIS-inspired.

articles.title: University of Alabama student James Gracey disappears in Barcelona after visiting friends studying abroad
articles.summary: University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey, 20, missing in Barcelona after visiting nightclub during spring break trip. Last seen 3 a.m. March 17.

articles.title: Masked suspect seen at Nancy Guthrie's home three weeks before her abduction, sources say
articles.summary: New details reveal a masked suspect was seen at Nancy Guthrie's home weeks before the apparent abduction of Savannah Guthrie's mother in Arizona.

articles.title: Mullin faces Democrat grilling in first hurdle to lead DHS and more top headlines
articles.summary: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

articles.title: FBI rescues missing teen from Florida hotel after month-long hunt for accused kidnapper
articles.summary: FBI rescues missing 16-year-old Ohio girl after month-long search, arresting Tennessee suspect accused of kidnapping in coordinated multistate operation.

articles.title: Pittsburgh police officers accused of ignoring ICE agents’ struggle with suspect
articles.summary: Pittsburgh police were allegedly told to stand down as ICE agents struggled with a suspect during a detention attempt. The chief has launched an investigation into the claims.

articles.title: Upscale shopping district rocked by alleged antisemitic beating as lawyer, 2 others charged
articles.summary: California attorney among three men charged in alleged antisemitic assault at Santana Row shopping center after Hebrew-speaking victims targeted in attack.

articles.title: Florida executes convict in 2008 rape, killing of woman, whose final call sparked 911 reforms
articles.summary: Florida executed Michael Lee King for the 2008 murder of Denise Amber Lee, who called 911 begging for help while tied up in his car during the crime.

articles.title: Republicans push campus carry laws in nearly a dozen states as college shootings reignite debate
articles.summary: The college gun debate intensifies as lawmakers in eight states weigh campus carry bills. Critics cite safety risks, while supporters argue for armed citizens.

articles.title: Florida high school student survives multiple injuries after Colorado spring break skiing disaster
articles.summary: Zoey McVoy is recovering in Denver after undergoing multiple surgeries for life-threatening injuries sustained during a skiing accident in Colorado.

articles.title: Comedy club owner with terminal cancer guns down ex-in-law, hunts ex-wife before turning up dead

articles.title: 23 arrested in alleged panga illegal immigrant smuggling plot off California coast
articles.summary: U.S. Customs and Border Protection intercepted a panga-style boat near San Clemente Island on Saturday, and 23 people were detained and turned over to ICE.

articles.title: Georgia cybercriminal allegedly duped NBA, NFL players in twisted phishing scam turned sex trafficking plot
articles.summary: A convicted cybercriminal is accused of escalating from phishing pro athletes to allegedly running disturbing sex trafficking operation while in federal prison.
